ab: Mangaay hakiya anu tara kaku a milisu’ i tisuwan?
ch: A:我可以拜訪你嗎?
en: A: May I visit you?

ab: Hay, kapah tu!
ch: B:可以，我很願意。
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: Mangaay hakiya anu tara kaku a milisu’ i tisuwan?
ch: A:我可以訪問你嗎?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: A caay kaku, caay kaadihay ku nikavana’an nu maku isaw.
ch: B:不要，我所知道的不多。
en: B:Don’t, I don’t know much about it.

ab: Mangalay isaw kaku a milisu’ i tisuwan, silawad hakiya kisu i herek nu kalahukan?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I’d like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: Hayi! Talaay tu nu maku kisu i luma’.
ch: B:好，我會在家等你。
en: B: Okay, I’ll wait for you at home.

ab: Silawad haw kisu anucila? Ira isaw ku papalitaan nu maku tu rayray nu Pangcah.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於阿美族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I would like to ask you about the legends of the Amis.

ab: A talalutuk kaku anucila, a awaay i luma’ isaw.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I’m going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won’t be home.

ab: Matesek ku nikavana’an nu misu tu lekakawa nu Pangcah saan ku nikatengil nu maku. Sisa, mangalay isaw kaku a mipalita i tisuwan.
ch: A:我聽說您對阿美族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you know a lot about Amis traditions and customs, so I would like to ask you a few questions.

ab: Caay kahacuwa ku nikavana’ nu maku, nika, sadakadakay hanaw tu nu maku a pacauv.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don’t know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: Kapah tu haw a lu-inen nu maku? Hangay maangaay a duduen a misurit i nalikulan.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: Hayi! Kapah tu!
ch: B:好的，沒問題。
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: Pinatu saw ku mihcaan nu misu anini?
ch: A:請問您今年幾歲了?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: Siwatu a pulu’ ira ku walu ku mihcaan nu maku.
ch: B:我已經98歲了。
en: B: I’m 98 years old.

ab: I terungay hen a miheca a melawen ku tireng isu haw.
ch: A:您看似中年的歲數唷!
en: A: You look middle-aged!

ab: Icuwa saw! Mavakitu kaku ira!
ch: B:沒有啦!我已經老了。
en: B: No, I’m not! I’m old.

ab: Icuwaay saw a niyaru’ kisu?
ch: A:請問您來自哪個部落?
en: A: What tribe do you come from?

ab: U Naturanay kaku.
ch: B:我來自娜荳蘭部落。
en: B: I’m from the Nadulan tribe.

ab: U Pangcah tu haw ku vavahi nu misu?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是阿美族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also [Amis]?

ab: Caay, u Puyuma cira.
ch: B:不，她是卑南族人。
en: B: No, she’s a Puyuma.

ab: U macuwaay saw a sasuwalen ku kavana’an nu misu?
ch: A:您會說哪些語言?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: Mavana’ kaku a semuwal tu nu Pangcah atu nu Puyuma isaw.
ch: B: 我會說阿美語和卑南語。
en: B: I can speak Ami and Puyuma.

ab: Maulah kaku anini a remiad a milisu’ i tisuwan, kedamay kasemuwal may tu paysin i nipiadupan namu?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，你們在狩獵時的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It’s a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboos you have when it comes to hunting?

ab: Hayi, kapah tu! Yu masakapah hen hinatayra kami i lutuk a miadup.
ch: B:好的。我們年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. We used to go hunting in the mountains when we were young.

ab: Tadamaan ku nipasevana’ nu misu itina remiadan, aray han nu maku kisu.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: Nu namaka tuasay sulinayay amin ku nipakungkuan nu maku.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: Hatilaaw tu ku nikasasuwal nu mita, awaay tu ku sasuwalen tu nipavatis nu misu, aray han nu maku kisu.
ch: A:我們今天的訪談就到這裡。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: This is the end of our interview today. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: Aka haeni ku suwal, rumaaw tu kita a masasuwal.
ch: B:不要這麼說，我們下次再聊。
en: B: Don’t say that, let’s talk about it next time.

ab: Hatilaaw tu ku demak ita, halhalay tu saadihayaw tu ku nipakungku anuruma.
ch: A:我們工作就到此，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: That’s all we’ve done. We look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: Hayi tu! naun han tu ku rakat.
ch: B:好喔!請慢走。
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
